purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: All tracked plugin source and configuration
outputs: Corrected plugin source and regression tests
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
All schemas and JSON files parse and cross-file version fields agree
CLI commands and failure paths behave deterministically in isolated temporary projects
Local personal-plugin reinstall exposes the same version and source behavior as the repository
Strict story gate passes with canonical evidence

notes: Independent audit lanes inherit the main Codex thread settings.

contextualization.summary: Audit and harden the complete Agentic SDLC Codex plugin. Inspect every tracked source, schema, template, test, document, packaging file, installer path, and relevant Git/SDLC evidence; reproduce defects before fixing them; preserve generic configurable behavior; add regression coverage; validate source and installed plugin; then commit and push main.

Create an SDLC contract for the next phase. Check the SDLC gate for my active story. brandColor #0EA5E9

# Agentic SDLC Codex Plugin Agentic SDLC is a Codex plugin that turns a classic software development life cycle into a contract-driven agent-legible operating system. The plugin gives Codex a reusable SDLC skill and a cross-platform Node CLI. The CLI creates a shared `.sdlc/` knowledge base inside the target project so teams and agents can work in parallel through Git branches and pull requests. ## What It Implements - Phase contracts for Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release. - Existing-project onboarding with an inferred baseline that must be confirmed before it becomes canonical. - A Git-first knowledge base for requirements stories decisions assumptions risks tests traces and releases. - Story-scoped workspaces so multiple agents can work in parallel without overwriting each other. - Work breakdown agreements for project-local epics stories tasks and approved decomposition choices. - Contextual capability discovery for project/story profiles skill/MCP/tool/model recommendations install approvals and technical decision matrices. - Contract capability policies for agreed skills MCPs tools bindings permissions and approval boundaries. - Approved dependenc

If a co

--- name agentic-sdlc description Use this skill when a user wants to run a contract-driven agentic SDLC in Codex initialize or maintain a shared project knowledge base create SDLC phase contracts split work into story-scoped parallel tracks validate gates capture decisions/tests/traces or use the Agentic SDLC plugin in a software project. --- # Agentic SDLC ## Purpose Use this skill to operate a stateless contract-driven SDLC for a target project. The plugin contains process templates schemas and CLI automation; all contracts output contracts traces and knowledge base artifacts must be saved inside the target project's `.sdlc/` directory. ## Core Rule Never store project contracts or project KB state inside the plugin installation. Treat the plugin as reusable method code only. Treat `<target-project>/.sdlc/` as the project source of truth. Treat `<target-project>/.sdlc/cache/` and `<target-project>/.sdlc/indexes/` as derived local optimization artifacts never as canonical evidence. ## Workflow 1. Identify the target project root. Default to the current workspace root unless the user names another project. 2. If `.sdlc/project.json` is missing initialize the project ```bash node <

#!/usr/bin/env python3 Stage Agentic SDLC into the default personal Codex marketplace. from __future__ import annotations import fnmatch import json import os import secrets import shutil import sys import tempfile import time from contextlib import contextmanager from pathlib import Path PurePosixPath PLUGIN_NAME = agentic-sdlc-codex-plugin DEFAULT_MARKETPLACE_NAME = personal STANDARD_ROOT_FILES = ( package.json README.md LICENSE ) EXCLUDED_NAMES = frozenset( .git .sdlc test .DS_Store ) EXCLUDED_FILE_SUFFIXES = ( .pyc .pyo ) INSTALL_LOCK_WAIT_SECONDS = 30.0 INSTALL_LOCK_STALE_SECONDS = 300.0 class InstallError(RuntimeError) Raised when staging cannot proceed without risking unmanaged files. def _lexists(path Path) -> bool return os.path.lexists(path) def _home_directory() -> Path configured_home = os.environ.get( HOME ) home = Path(configured_home).expanduser() if configured_home else Path.home() if not home.is_absolute() raise InstallError(f HOME must be an absolute path got home ) return home def _assert_no_nested_symlinks(base Path target Path label str) -> None try relative = target.absolute().relative_to(base.absolute()) except ValueError as exc raise InstallError(f label esc

questions.question: What is the requested outcome?
questions.answer: A fully audited, corrected, tested, reinstalled, committed, and pushed plugin with no known in-scope defects left unexplained

questions.question: Which sources are authoritative?
questions.answer: All tracked repository files, current Git history, the approved 2026-07-10 baseline, and canonical project-local SDLC records; derived cache and index files are not evidence

questions.question: What may Codex do autonomously?
questions.answer: Read and edit this repository, run local non-destructive checks, create temporary test projects, use independent audit agents, reinstall this local personal plugin, and stage, commit, and push main

questions.question: What remains outside scope?
questions.answer: Production deploys, secret access, destructive Git history, unrelated repositories, and unrequested external services

questions.question: What durable output is required?
questions.answer: An implementation summary linked through implementation-summary-v1 plus test, gate, release, and Git sync evidence in .sdlc

questions.question: What proves completion?
questions.answer: All acceptance criteria pass; syntax, full E2E, negative paths, package contents, local install, installed CLI, manifest/version parity, strict SDLC gate, commit, and origin/main push are verified

approvals.summary: Antonio requested autonomous whole-plugin analysis, correction, testing, reinstall, commit, and push; this final refresh includes the generated-plan regression inside the same scope.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: whole-plugin audit, implementation, validation, reinstall, release evidence, commit, and push
